title: Reassembling the Mercedes F1 Wheel
prompt: Walk me through reassembling this steering wheel.

explanation.intentSummary: Guide the user through an assembly procedure.
explanation.written: The prompt 'Walk me through reassembling this steering wheel' is asking for a guided assembly. A good answer is a step-by-step sequence: ghost the next part to show where it's going to land, commit it on advance, name the part + its function, and end with a side-by-side before/after so the user can confirm the procedure landed.

steps.instruction: Drop the hub into the central spline; align the index mark.
Bolt the lower rim bar across the bottom of the hub.
Slide the left grip onto the lower rim and torque to spec.
Slide the right grip on; check the paddle ribbon is seated.
Click each top control bay into place; cable routing on top, not pinched.
Drop the centre display into its frame; the LCD ribbon plugs into the hub.
Thread the quick-release nut; the Mercedes star is the visible face.

mechanics.why: Build the wheel step-by-step so the user can follow along.

items.label: Hub
items.finding: Splines onto the column. The wheel's reference frame starts here.

items.label: Lower rim bar
items.finding: Cross-piece tying the two grips into one composite chassis.

items.label: Left grip
items.finding: Suede-wrapped handle the driver's left hand never leaves.

items.label: Right grip
items.finding: Right-hand grip; DRS button often lives on the back of this one.

mechanics.why: Label each part with its name + function as it's committed.
Confirm the procedure landed by comparing the parts-apart and reassembled states.

sources.name: Mercedes F1 Steering Wheel
sources.finding: Hero. Sits on the workbench so the assembly sequence reads cleanly from above.
